I will call a small/medium river bet but fold to heavy pressure that prices out my draw/value.

On this river, I have Ten-high and no showdown value. However, the action has gone check-check on both the flop and the turn on a very coordinated board. While I could attempt a bluff to represent a weak Ace or a slow-played flush, the pot is relatively small (600 chips), and my opponent's checks could signify a marginal made hand (like a small pair or a weak Ace) that they are simply trying to get to showdown with. Given my significant chip lead and the fact that I've shown no interest in the pot since pre-flop, I will check and concede the hand if Minni has any pair, rather than risking more chips on a low-frequency bluff.

Flash was the preflop aggressor but checked flop, turn and river, showing weakness/pot control. I have only a small pair (4s) with a Jack kicker and missed my club draw on the river. Pot is small (600) and there’s no reason to bluff into a checked pot out of position — betting would gain little and could get me raised by hands that already beat me. Best line is pot control and take this to showdown: check and see the showdown. I would call a small river bet but fold to heavy pressure.

I am on the button with Queen-Five offsuit (Q5o). While opening ranges from the button are generally wide in short-handed play, Q5o is a marginal hand that often underperforms, especially if either Minni or Elon decide to defend or 3-bet. Given the recent history of aggression from both opponents, I will choose the conservative route and fold this weak hand to wait for better opportunities.

I have 3c2h — a very weak, low-card hand with minimal showdown value. Facing only the big blind and needing to put in 50 more chips (to make 100 total) the price is cheap: calling 50 into a 150 pot gives ≈25% pot equity requirement. I won't raise such a garbage hand; that would be overplaying and could get me into a big pot out of line. Folding would forfeit a very small price to see a flop where I can leverage position and fold to aggression. Plan: complete to 100 to see a flop cheaply, re-evaluate based on flop texture and Elon's betting; fold to a sizable raise preflop or a strong line that says I'm beaten.

Preflop in the big blind with Jc6c (suited connector, speculative hand). Flash has folded, so heads-up versus Minni who limped from the small blind (wide range likely, often weak or trapping). Pot is 200 with 0 to call, excellent implied odds to see a flop cheaply and realize equity postflop where this hand plays well (flush/straight potential). Raising has some steal equity but J6s is not strong enough for consistent value raises against a limper; better to check and pot control/see flop in position postflop. Minni's history shows aggression but limps suggest weakness here. Long-term EV favors checking.

I hold 3c2h — essentially junk. The flop Kd 9c Th misses me completely: no pair, no meaningful backdoor straight/flush prospects. I’m out of position to Elon (BB) and the pot is small relative to stacks. Betting as a bluff here is low-expected-value (he’ll continue with many decent hands or call lightly), so I’ll exercise pot control. Check and fold to any real aggression. If Elon checks back I can see a free turn and re-evaluate; I’ll only consider calling an extremely tiny probe but will fold to any standard c-bet sized attack.

Heads-up vs Minni who limped from SB (wide, weak range often) and checked flop (polarized: weak hands or monsters, but limpers rarely trap). Flop Kd9cTh is decent for her range (Kx, Tx possible) but drawy. My Jc6c is a strong nut club flush draw (~10 outs, 35-40% equity vs calling range) with good playability and implied odds deep-stacked. OOP, checking risks giving free card to her equity or letting her realize hers cheaply; betting semi-bluffs to take down weak hands, build pot for when I hit, deny her equity (straight/flush draws), and fold out better hands like weak Kx or Tx. Pot 200, bet ~60% pot for good fold equity without overbetting. History shows Minni aggressive postflop but passive here preflop; exploit by firing. Long-term +EV.

Hand is junk (3c2h) — completely missed the Kd-9c-Th flop with no pair and no meaningful backdoors. Elon’s c-bet to 125 into the ~200 pot (pot now 325) is ~62% pot-size; calling 125 to win 325 requires ~27.8% equity. My hand’s equity vs a standard c-betting range (Kx, Tx, 9x, broadways, some draws) is far below that (single-digit to low-teens at best). I’m out of position and have no realistic fold equity or implied odds to justify a marginal call. Stick to plan: fold and preserve stack for better spots.

I'm on the button with A♥2♣ — a hand that's worth opening for position and steal equity. The pot is 150 and calling 100 invites multiway action where A2o is often dominated; a standard 3x BB open to 300 gives decent fold equity, keeps pots manageable, and plays well heads-up in position. Elon (SB, ~85BB) and Flash (BB, deep) may call or 3‑bet; 300 is a natural table size that pressures the blinds without overcommitting. I'll open-raise to 300 to try to take the blinds now and preserve position postflop against callers.

3h5h is a marginal suited connector but too weak and gapped for SB defense vs button raise. Minni likely has a wide range but still ahead. OOP post-flop with poor playability and low equity multiway potential vs Flash squeeze. Better to fold and wait for stronger hands to maximize stack long-term.

I am in the Big Blind holding Qd 6s. Minni opened to 300 from the Button and Elon folded. Q6o is a weak hand that does not play well out of position against a 3x open. While the pot odds are roughly 2:1, the lack of connectivity and the disadvantage of being out of position throughout the hand make this a standard fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

3-handed preflop on the button with SB folded and BB checked (limped pot). Pot has 150 including dead SB money. T2o is a marginal hand but in position vs BB, raising wide is standard to steal blinds and realize equity postflop. History shows I frequently raise to 300 from button and take it down. BB defends selectively but folds often to button aggression. Raise to 300 (3x BB) for value, fold equity, and position. If called, play postflop IP with decent blocker (T). EV+ play deepstacked.

I am in the Small Blind with K5o. The Button (Elon) has opened for 3x. K5o is too weak to call out of position, and it's not a strong enough candidate for a 3-bet bluff against this specific opponent who has shown frequent opening aggression. The best play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m in the big blind with Q♠3♦ facing a button open to 300 (I’ve already put 100 in). Q3o is a weak, offsuit, often dominated hand with very poor postflop playability out of position. Calling 200 to defend gives ~30.7% break-even equity, but versus a button’s opening range my realized equity and postflop edge are likely worse. A 3-bet bluff OOP with this holding is thin and unnecessary with deep stacks — better to conserve chips and wait for a better spot. Fold.

I am on the button with 4h 5h, which are low suited connectors. This is a standard hand to open with a raise from the button to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot with position. A standard 3x raise to 300 is appropriate here.

Facing a button open to 3bb from Flash, I'm in the small blind with 9♦7♥ offsuit. To call I must add 250 into a 450 pot, which requires ~36% equity — far more than 97o typically has versus a button raising range. The hand is frequently dominated and has poor postflop playability out of position, and a tiny 3-bet to 500 would be a thin bluff that's easy for the BB to squeeze or the button to call/4-bet. With deep stacks I should conserve chips and choose better spots with hands that realize equity or have better blockers. Fold here.

64o is a complete trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. Facing a button raise after SB folds, Flash's range is wide but still far stronger on average than my holding. Defending wide in BB is a losing strategy long-term, especially deepstacked. No position postflop, low equity against his continuing range. Best to fold and wait for better spots to realize equity.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed pot with K♠T♣. In heads-up/3-way play this is a standard open — it’s ahead of many blind defending ranges and plays well post‑flop. Both opponents have shown aggression but also wide opening/calling ranges; I want to seize the initiative, isolate, and get value from worse hands. A 3x open (300 total) is a standard sizing here: it gives good fold equity, keeps weaker holdings out, and keeps SPR reasonable with deep stacks. Plan: fold (or seriously consider folding) to a large 3‑bet given KTo’s vulnerability; if called, C-bet favorable K/T/A texture flops and control the pot on coordinated/dangerous boards.

9d4s is an extremely weak hand with no playability, connectivity, or high card strength. Facing a raise to 300 from Minni on the button, where she has position and a wide range, calling from the small blind out of position would be a significant mistake. My stack is 8500, pot 450, to call 250 – poor odds for trash. Folding preserves chips for better opportunities in this long 150-hand session. Historical tendencies show Minni raises frequently, but no need to defend this.

I am in the Big Blind with 8d 2d. Minni raised to 300 from the Button. Although the hand is suited, it is very weak and I will be playing out of position for the rest of the hand. Given the 3x raise size, I do not have the necessary equity or playability to justify a call here.

3-handed on the button facing a checked big blind after SB likely folded (pot 150, to call 100). T2 suited is playable with position and potential for flush/straight draws. Minni has shown aggression but checked here, indicating weakness. Raising to steal the pot or build it while maintaining initiative post-flop. Standard sizing around 3x BB.

I am in the Small Blind with 6h 2s, which is a very weak hand. Elon has raised from the Button to 300. Defending such a weak hand out of position is not profitable in the long run, especially against a standard button open. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with Q♦9♣ — a playable hand heads-up versus a wide opening range. Pot is 450 and I need to call 200 (300 total), which gives me ~31% break-even equity; Q9o has reasonable equity and good postflop playability heads-up. I don’t want to 3-bet with this marginal offsuit Queen — a min 3-bet to 500 has little fold equity and a larger 3-bet commits me OOP with a hand that is often dominated. With deep stacks (effective ≈82bb) I’ll defend by calling and see a flop, folding to heavy action if I miss and face significant aggression, but continuing on top pair/strong draws.

I completely missed this A‑8‑4 rainbow with Qd9c — no pair and only a very weak backdoor club. Elon opened from the button, so his continuing range contains a lot of Ax and many hands that dominate my Q9o. Betting OOP as a bluff is thin here (he can call with many better hands), and I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position with a marginal hand. Plan: control the pot by checking; fold to a standard/large c‑bet, and only consider calling a very small probe bet to float if I have a genuine read he bluffs frequently. If he checks back I get a free turn to reevaluate. Action: check.

Preflop raiser on the button facing a check from Minni in BB after calling my raise. Flop A-8-4 rainbow is a good c-bet board: ace high, dry, disconnected. My T2c has backdoor club flush draw and straight potential (gutshot to wheel-ish if 3/5 comes, or broadway backdoors). No showdown value, but position is excellent. Minni's history shows she calls pre but folds to postflop aggression often (e.g., Hands 2,3,13,14,19). C-bet sizing ~60% pot (400) to apply pressure, deny equity to her floats/dominates, and maintain initiative. Pot control not ideal with position; betting extracts value from worse or folds out better one time.

Flop A♦‑8♣‑4♠, I hold Q♦9♣ and I checked to keep the pot under control. Elon c‑bets 400 into a 1,050 pot — calling 400 makes the pot ~1,450, so I need ~27.6% equity to justify a call. I have no pair and essentially no realistic draws (I can't make a five‑card flush by the river with the remaining two cards); only 6 immediate outs to a pair and many of those will still be second‑best. BTN opening and c‑betting range contains lots of Ax, 8x/4x, overpairs and strong broadway combos that dominate me, and raising as a bluff OOP here has very thin fold equity and would overcommit with poor blockers. With negative expected value to continue, I fold.

4-5 suited is a solid speculative hand to open with from the button in a 3-handed game. I have a significant stack advantage over both opponents. Raising to 300 is consistent with my previous pre-flop opening sizes and allows me to take the initiative. If one of them calls, the hand has good post-flop playability through straights and flushes.

I’m in the small blind with 2♣3♠ — a very weak, offsuit, unconnected hand with almost no playability out of position. Flash opened to 300 from the button and Elon (BB) still has to act; calling 250 into a 450 pot gives me only ~2.8:1 pot odds (need ~26–27% equity) but 2‑3o has far less equity vs a typical button open range and performs poorly multiway or vs a possible 3‑bet. A 3‑bet bluff here is unattractive (no blockers, out of position, exploitable if called), and calling leads to difficult postflop decisions out of position for a marginal hand. With a 110 BB stack I’d prefer to preserve chips for better spots. Fold.

Facing a standard button raise to 3x (300) from Flash, who has shown aggression from late position in prior hands. In BB with K6d suited: king-high suited hand with flush potential and decent blocker to KK. Pot odds excellent: calling 200 more (total 300) into 450 pot requires ~31% equity, which K6s exceeds vs. typical button opening range (~45% equity). Playable OOP with straight/flush draws possible. Folding too tight for defense strategy; 3-bet OOP marginal with this hand, better to call and realize equity on flops where we hit or float. Deep stacks allow post-flop maneuvering.

Flop brings a powerful flush draw (9 diamond outs, ~35% equity by river) with king-high and backdoor straight potential on a coordinated Ad4d9c board heavy with diamonds. As BB OOP first to act vs button raiser Flash, who is aggressive preflop, standard play is to check to induce a continuation bet from his wide range. This sets up profitable check-calls (good pot odds) or check-raises as semi-bluffs to build the pot with our strong equity, deny equity to his floats/draws, and fold out worse ace-high hands. Leading out (donk) risks facing a raise OOP, complicating postflop play on deep stacks. Checking maintains balance in our BB defending range and realizes equity optimally over 150-hand horizon.

I have bottom pair with a five kicker on an Ace-high board. As the pre-flop aggressor, I have the range advantage, but bottom pair is a marginal hand that is better played for pot control. By checking back, I realize my equity and keep the pot small while also protecting my checking range. I can re-evaluate on the turn based on the card and Elon's action.

Turn pairs the 4, board A♦9♣4♦4♣ features a paired low card, dampening some straight draws but my diamond flush draw remains live (9 outs, ~19% to hit river). King-high with backdoor potential holds up vs many bluffs/misses in Flash's button range. Check-check flop suggests he missed his equity-heavy hands or has a trapping monster (unlikely given aggression profile). OOP as BB defender, leading with a donk bet semi-bluffs effectively: denies equity to his floats/draws (e.g., lower diamonds, gutshots), extracts value from worse Ax (A-high, weak kickers), builds pot for when we hit flush, and applies fold equity post his flop check (range skews weaker). Size 450 (~2/3 pot) balances value/semi-bluff in deep-stack spot vs aggressive button. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/draws or facing tough river spots OOP. High-EV line over long run.

I have improved to trips on the turn with the Four of Clubs. After checking back the flop to control the pot with bottom pair, the turn has made my hand significantly stronger. Elon's lead of 450 into a 650 pot suggests he either has an Ace, a Nine, or is attempting to take the pot down after my flop check. By calling rather than raising, I keep his range wide, allowing him to continue bluffing on the river or to value-bet a weaker Ace. Raising would likely only get called by hands that have me beat (like A4 or a better 4) or very strong Aces, whereas calling maximizes my value against his entire range while I have position.

River 8♦ completes the nut flush for me with K♦6♦ (A♦K♦8♦6♦4♦), the absolute nuts barring straight flush (impossible). Board paired 4s dampens some draws, but Flash's button calling range (preflop raise, flop check to my draw, turn call of 450 into ~1100 pot) includes value like AT-AJ, A9, 44, 94, 84, weaker diamond draws (QJ-75d for flush), sets (99, AA unlikely pre), two-pair combos, and bluffs/floats. Deep stacks (8k effective), pot 1550: optimal line is value bet sizing for calls from Ax (weak kickers beatable), two-pair, smaller flushes (if holding lower diamonds like 7d5d etc., though board-heavy), and traps like slowplayed A4. Size 1100 (~70% pot) maximizes EV: thin value from worse + fold equity vs pure air/missed draws, denies check-behind, balances with our turn bet range (draws that missed/bricked). Checking risks free showdown to worse or hero-call; overbet unnecessary vs aggressive button who calls wide postflop (history: Hand20 raise, Hand22 multiway checks, Hand13 call then fold). High-EV thin value over 150 hands.

I have trips (Fours) on a board that ended with a flush possibility. On the turn, I called Elon's lead to keep his bluffs in. The river is the 8d, completing the diamond flush. Elon bets 1100 into 1550 (approx 70% pot). Given my flop check-back, my hand is somewhat under-represented, but the board is now quite dangerous with the flush completing and the Ace being a very common card in his range. However, trips are generally too strong to fold in a 3-handed game against a single opponent bet, even with the flush draw getting there. Elon could easily be value-betting an Ace (like AJ/AT/A9) or even a Nine, or bluffing. Raising is risky because it likely only gets called by flushes, full houses, or better trips (like A4, though I hold a 4). Calling is the most profitable action as it captures value from his bluffs and thin value bets while losing the minimum if he actually has the flush.

A♦T♠ on the button is a clear open: good blockers, strong connectivity, and excellent positional leverage against the blinds. I want to take the initiative and reduce multiway pots, so I’ll raise to a standard 3x BB (300) — bigger than the min-raise to widen fold equity and match table open-sizing I’ve observed. If called I’ll play postflop in position (c-bet on A-high and dry boards, pot-control or check when out of position/heavily contested); if I face a small 3-bet (~600) I’ll consider flatting to realize equity, but fold to very large 3-bets/4-bets. This sizing maximizes my expected value versus these stacks and tendencies.

Preflop in SB with K9o facing a button raise to 300 (needing 250 more to call into 450 pot). K9o is offsuit, marginal hand with poor postflop playability OOP vs a likely wide button range from Minni. BB (Flash) still to act and could squeeze or call wide. Pot odds are about 1.8:1 but not enough equity OOP against raiser's range. Standard fold; no value in calling or 3-betting.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ace-Four offsuit (Ac 4d). Minni, on the Button, has raised to 300, and Elon has folded. My hand is definitely strong enough to defend against a standard button raise. Calling is the most standard play here; it allows me to see a flop with a hand that has good high-card value and nut-flush potential (though only for the Ace in this case), while keeping the pot manageable out of position. Raising is also an option but with A4o, I prefer to keep the pot small and see if I hit an Ace or a straight draw.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Four offsuit. The flop is Nine-King-Three with two diamonds. I have an Ace high, which is sometimes the best hand but very vulnerable on this texture. Minni, who raised pre-flop, will often have a King or a better Ace. Since I am out of position and the board doesn't provide me with a strong draw or a pair, I will check to see how Minni acts. If Minni bets, I will likely fold unless the bet is very small, as Ace-high has limited showdown value here.

I opened A♦T♠ from the button and the big blind just called. Flop K♦ 9♠ 3♦ checked to me. I’m out of position vs a single defender but this is still a good spot to take the initiative. I have the A♦ blocker (reduces opponent Ax combos and some strong diamond combos), two overcards to the 9 and 3, and backdoor diamond/straight possibilities. The board is relatively dry and the BB’s defending range is wide, so a well-sized c-bet will fold lots of unmade hands and charge draws. I’ll bet roughly half the pot to balance fold equity and value (350 into 650). Plan: if called I’ll re-evaluate the turn and continue only on favorable runouts (A/T or blanks that fit my perceived range); I will be ready to concede to significant aggression since I currently only have Ace-high.

I am holding Ace-high (Ac 4d) on a board of Kd 9s 3d. Minni, who raised pre-flop from the button, has followed up with a continuation bet of 350 into a 650 pot. This board texture favors her opening range, which includes many Kings, Nines, and diamond draws. While Ace-high can sometimes be the best hand if she is bluffing with lower cards, I am out of position with very little equity to improve. I don't have a flush draw or a straight draw to justify a float. Folding is the most disciplined and prudent play here to preserve my stack.

Unopened pot on the button with 3 active players, effectively short-handed. A5o is a strong enough hand to open from the button due to excellent position, ace-high blocking value, straight potential, and overall playability postflop. Standard open-raise size of 300 (3x BB) to build the pot, take initiative, and apply pressure on the blinds' wide defending ranges. Consistent with observed opening sizes in previous hands.

I am in the Small Blind with 9c 7d, which is a weak offsuit hand. Elon has raised from the Button to 300. Defending such a mediocre hand out of position against a Button open is generally unprofitable in the long run. I have a healthy stack and see no reason to play a marginal hand out of position here.

I’m in the big blind with T♥9♥ facing a button open to 300. This is a very defendable, multi-street hand against a wide BTN opening range — good equity and strong turn/river playability with deep (~113BB) stacks. I prefer to call (total 300) to keep the pot controlled and realize equity rather than 3-betting small (which often just gets called) or bloating the pot out of position. Plan: see the flop and continue with top-pair/strong draws; fold or pot-control vs heavy aggression on dry boards.

Check. I have top pair (T9) on a very dry T-7-4 rainbow. I’m out of position vs a wide BTN opener and stacks are deep (~111BB), so pot control is priority — checking induces c-bets/bluffs and keeps worse hands in while avoiding bloating the pot with a marginal kicker. Plan: call small/standard c-bets and reevaluate on the turn; if he checks back I can consider a small delayed value bet on safe turns or size up for value on improving turns. Fold to clear polarization/huge raises unless the turn texture and reads justify calling/raising.

In position on the button versus the big blind defender after a standard button open that she called flat preflop. The flop 7s4cTd is dry and uncoordinated (rainbow, low cards, no flush draws started, limited straight draws like 65 OESD or J9/85 guts). My As5c is ace-high with no pair but solid blockers to strong Ax hands (AA, AK, A10, ATs) in my perceived opening range. Minni's check-first suggests weakness, folding out better than villain pairs/draws often, and her history shows she checks weak holdings postflop after calling pre. Standard c-bet here to apply pressure, deny equity to weaker hands/draws, take down the pot ~60-70% of the time given position and board texture. Sizing ~50% pot (325-350) balances value (overpairs, sets, T7/T4) with bluffs like this, maintaining range advantage. Pot control if checked, but betting exploits position and initiative. Long-term EV positive in 150-hand game.

Call 350. I have top pair (T♥9♥) with a decent but non-nut kicker on a very dry T-7-4 rainbow. Elon is a BTN opener who will c-bet a wide range; his flop bet is small (~350 into 1000, ~35% pot), so calling is cheap and keeps worse hands and bluffs in the pot. Raising out of position would bloat the pot with a marginal kicker and often commit me against stronger tens/sets/7x hands, while folding is too weak. Plan: control the pot by calling and re-evaluate on the turn — if he checks I can extract thin value or bet small; if he barrels large or polarizes I will fold unless I improve or pot odds justify a call.

I have T♥9♥ on a T♦7♠4♣ turn that paired the Ten (Td, Tc on board) — I’ve made trips. This is a very strong but somewhat vulnerable hand: I can be behind to Tens with a bigger kicker or crushed by full houses/quads (pocket 77/44 or T7/T4/TT), but those combos are relatively uncommon. Elon checked the turn, which often indicates medium strength or a checking-range rather than a locked-up full house; checking behind surrenders value and gives a free river to many turn/river bluff-catchers. I want to extract thin value from worse Tx, medium pairs, and weaker holdings while charging any floaters, so I’ll fire a value bet of roughly half the pot (~700 into 1350). If he raises large on the turn I’ll strongly consider folding (or re-evaluating if the raise is small), but if he calls I’ll re-evaluate river for further thin value or pot control depending on the river card.

Facing a turn lead of 700 into 2050 from Minni (BB OOP) on a 7s4cTdTc board after she check-called flop c-bet with As5c (ace-high, no pair/draw). The paired 10s improve her calling range (TT, T7s/T9s/T4s, sets, two-pair combos) significantly while bricking mine completely—no showdown value, no straight/flush draw (gutshot unlikely vs her range), pure float gone wrong. Her flop check-call suggests mid-strength like T9-J9, 87, Ax weak; turn lead reps polarization: strong value (trips+/full houses unlikely but possible) or bluffs (missed draws like J9 OESD now dead, 86, flush draws bricked). However, leading into raiser on paired board skews toward value-heavy (defending range now bets thin value/protection), especially given pot size and her history of postflop aggression/value-betting (e.g., Hand 13 turn bet, Hand 14 c-bets, Hand 17/19 pots won). Pot odds ~3.9:1 (700 to 2750) require ~20% equity, but ace-high crushes <15% vs polarized lead range (loses to all value, races some bluffs). Folding preserves deep 10k stack for 120+ hands, avoids spew on texture where villain exploits positionally weak floats. Long-term EV: check-fold standard; calling commits to river stack-off OOP disadvantage without improvement. Consistent with GTO vs aggro defender.

I have KTo on the button. This is a standard opening hand in a 3-handed game. I want to put pressure on the blinds and potentially win the pot pre-flop or play a pot in position. Raising to 300 is a standard open size here.

I’m in the small blind with A♠Q♦ facing a button open to 300 in a 3-handed pot. AQo is too strong to limp/call out of position — calling hands me a multiway pot OOP and gives opponents good odds. I want to isolate the button and take the initiative, so I’ll 3-bet to 1,000 (≈3.3x the open). That size gives solid fold equity against a wide BTN range, extracts value from worse A/K and medium pairs, and is large enough to discourage light calls/squeezes. If I face a large 4-bet I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to shove-sized 4-bets from a tight player, call smaller 4-bets occasionally depending on who 4-bets).

86s is a speculative suited connector, but facing a button open-raise from Flash (wide range) and a sizable 3-bet to 1000 from Minni in SB (likely tighter range: JJ+, AKo, suited broadways, some bluffs). In BB OOP, calling 900 into 1400 pot requires ~39% equity multiway, which 86s lacks against their combined ranges (low equity vs value, draws unlikely to realize well OOP). Post-flop playability poor deepstacked OOP vs 3-bettor. No good blockers for 4-bet bluff squeeze. Opponents deep; folding preserves stack for better spots. History shows Minni aggressive but this spot too weak.

I opened to 300 from the button with King-Ten offsuit, and Minni 3-bet from the small blind to 1000. In a 3-handed game, KTo is a decent hand, but it performs poorly against a 3-bet range from the small blind, which typically consists of strong linear hands or well-constructed bluffs. Minni's stack is around 11k, and my position doesn't fully compensate for the range disadvantage here. Calling puts me in a difficult spot post-flop against a strong range, and 4-betting is too loose with this specific hand. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Three-handed on the button with K♥4♠ (K4o). With deep stacks (~125bb) and only the blinds to beat, button open-raise ranges should be wide — K4o is marginal but plays well in position and can often steal the blinds. I want fold equity and to take the initiative, so I’ll raise rather than limp. I’ll size to 300 (roughly 3x the big blind) to apply pressure and isolate heads-up. Plan: fold to a large preflop 3‑bet (K4o doesn’t justify a sticky defense), and if called use position to c-bet favorable flops or control the pot and pick spots postflop.

84dd is a speculative suited connector, but very weak and low-ranked. Facing a button raise from Minni, who frequently raises pre-flop (seen in hands 8,9,14,15,21,24,27,30), her range is wide but profitable for her in position. I'm OOP in SB, with poor post-flop playability multi-way or heads-up against a c-bettor. Pot odds to call 250 into 450 (~2:1) don't justify the equity needed (~25-30% implied) given reverse implied odds and position disadvantage. Deep stacks don't help this hand much OOP. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with 6h 7c. Minni has raised to 300 (3x) from the button. While 67o is a connected hand, it is quite weak for defending against a 3x open, especially since I will be out of position for the remainder of the hand. In a three-handed game, ranges are wider, but 67o still underperforms in equity realization from the blinds against a button open. I'll choose the disciplined fold here to wait for better spots.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed pot with SB folded. Facing the BB's 100 (to call 100 into 150). J5o is a weak offsuit hand with poor post-flop playability, dominated often, and low equity against BB's wide defending range. Deep stacks (~100BB), no need to risk chips with marginal hand. Standard play is to raise or fold from BTN vs BB; limping is passive and suboptimal. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace of Hearts and Eight of Clubs. Elon on the button has folded, leaving only me and the Big Blind (Minni) in the hand. A8o is a strong hand for a heads-up situation from the small blind. I will raise to 300 to take the initiative and either win the pot immediately or play the hand with range advantage against the Big Blind's defending range.

I will call the extra 200 (300 total) with A♠5♠ from the big blind. A5s is a strong defensive hand heads-up: it has good postflop playability and blocks some of SB’s nut-A combinations. Stacks are deep (~125bb), so flatting realizes equity and keeps the pot manageable out of position; Flash has been opening to 300 frequently, suggesting a fairly wide range I can exploit by seeing a flop. A small 3-bet is an option as a balance, but the +EV line here is to defend and play the flop rather than bloat the pot preflop.

I was the pre-flop aggressor and the board [Td, 2h, 2d] is relatively dry. While I only have Ace-high, this board doesn't hit the Big Blind's defending range very hard unless they have a Ten or a Deuce. I will place a continuation bet of half the pot to represent strength and try to take down the pot immediately.

Flop: Td 2h 2d. I hold A♠5♠. Flash opened from the SB fairly wide preflop and has been c-betting small often. This is a dry, paired board where my Ace-high has real showdown value and also blocks a chunk of his Ace-high combos. The bet is only 300 into a 900 pot (~1/3 pot) — calling 300 to win ~1,200 gives very good pot odds and keeps the pot controllable OOP with deep stacks (~120+ BB). Raising here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and isn’t necessary to fold out many better hands; folding would give up too much against a wide c-bet range. Plan: call the 300, reevaluate on the turn — fold to large, polarizing turn aggression unless I pair my Ace or get a clear read to continue; call small-medium turn bets to realize equity.

The turn card is the Ten of Spades, making the board Td-2h-2d-Ts. This is a double-paired board. My Ace-high (A8o) has decent showdown value if the hand goes to check-check, but I am likely behind if Minni has a Ten or a pocket pair. However, the board is very static. Minni called my flop bet, which could indicate a Ten, a pocket pair, or Ace-high. On this turn, the Tens and Deuces are mostly blocked or accounted for. I will check to see how Minni reacts and aim to realize my showdown value or potentially catch a bluff on the river, rather than continuing to bet into a range that called the flop.

Turn: Td Ts 2h 2d. Flash checked after c-betting the flop. My A♠5♠ has almost no showdown value on a double‑paired board — any Ten or Deuce in his range now crushes me. Flash’s check looks like weakness or pot control; I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal bluff that will get called by any Ten/Deuce or punished by a raise. I’ll check and let him show his hand or barrel the river; plan is to call small-to-medium river bets (reasonable pot odds) to realize any tiny equity but fold to large, polarizing river raises. Checking preserves stack and keeps options open rather than committing to a losing showdown.

The board is double-paired with Tens and Deuces. My hand (A8) is effectively Two Pair, Tens and Deuces, with an Ace kicker. However, on this board, any pocket pair held by Minni (33 through 99) would result in a better Two Pair (e.g., Tens and Fours if she has 44). Minni called the flop and checked the turn, suggesting she has some showdown value or a hand she's willing to take to the river. Since my Ace-high has some showdown value but is unlikely to be called by a worse hand if I bet, I will check to see the showdown as cheaply as possible.

Board is Td 2h 2d Ts 3c (double‑paired board). I hold A♠5♠ (no Ten/Deuce), pot is 1,200 and Flash (SB) checked river after a small flop c‑bet and checking the turn. His line polarizes to either a trap (T/2) or missed/medium hands. I have almost no showdown value vs the portion of his range that now contains full houses (any Ten or Deuce) and my Ace only blocks a few Ax combos. Betting here would be a thin bluff with limited fold equity and a large penalty if he raises or calls with a made hand. Optimal, low‑variance play is to check behind, preserve stack and either show down for the small pot or fold to any meaningful river aggression. If he leads a small sizing I can call to realize equity; I will fold to large, polarizing raises.

I am on the button with Q4o. While Q4o is a weak hand, it is sometimes used as a raise in a three-handed game to steal the blinds. However, given the table dynamics and the fact that both Minni and Elon have been active and occasionally aggressive, a fold is the safest and most disciplined play to avoid playing a weak hand out of position post-flop if called.

I’m in the small blind with K♦Q♠ (KQo) and only the big blind to act. This is a strong hand to take the initiative rather than just completing to 100 and playing out of position passively. I’ll make a small, constructive raise to 200 total — enough aggression to isolate the big blind and earn fold equity while keeping the pot reasonable with deep effective stacks. Plan: if Elon calls I’ll c-bet favorable flops and play carefully when out-kicked; if he 3-bets large I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to heavy pressure, call small 3-bets). This raise maximizes expected value and keeps options open postflop.

T9o is a decent speculative hand from the BB facing a small raise from the SB. Minni has been aggressive with preflop raises in previous hands but often shows down marginal holdings or folds to resistance. Pot odds are excellent: calling 100 more into a 300 pot gives over 3:1 direct odds, with significant implied odds due to deep stacks (98BB effective). T9o has good playability for straights and two-pair potential, and we can realize equity multiway or heads-up. Folding is too tight for BB defense range vs SB. Raising risks playing OOP postflop against a polarized range, so calling to control pot size and see flop is optimal.

Flop: Kc 7c 2c — I have K♠Q♦: top pair, decent kicker, but no club. This is a dangerous monotone board because the BB’s calling range (from cold-calling my SB open) contains a lot of suited hands and club combos that have already made a flush. Betting here would bloat the pot and get value mainly from Kx/pocket pairs while losing a lot to any club. I also block some K combos (I hold K♠) which slightly reduces opponent Kx frequency, but it does nothing to reduce flush combos.

Given deep stacks and the texture, I want pot control and information. I’ll check to avoid getting raised/offered difficult decisions with a marginally vulnerable hand. Plan: if Elon bets a small sizing (1/3–1/2 pot, ~100–200) I will lean toward a call to keep worse Kx and pocket pairs in and to re-evaluate on the turn; if he sizes large (close to pot or larger) I will respect it and fold to avoid committing with a hand that’s often crushed by a made flush. If he checks back I will take the turn for free and either barrel a safe non-club turn for value or continue pot control if a club or scary card comes.

On this flop of 2c-Kc-7c, a highly coordinated monotone board favoring flush draws and strong club hands, my T9o has zero equity: no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw. Minni's SB minraise preflop indicates a wide range including suited connectors, Ax suited, broadway, etc., many of which connect well here (e.g., Acx, Kc, suited broadway clubs). Her check as OOP suggests possible weakness (missed the board) or trapping with a monster, but betting into her with pure air is -EV given the board texture and her aggression history. Checking preserves equity realization on turn (possible runner-runner outs), controls the pot OOP for her future bets, and avoids bloating the pot with nothing. Bluffing is low frequency spot; better to check and reassess turn dynamics. Position advantage postflop allows cheap info.

Flop check was pot-control on a very club-heavy (Kc 7c 2c) board — I wanted to avoid getting raised off a marginal top pair. The 5♥ on the turn is a safe, non-club blank, so this is a good spot to charge worse Kx and pocket pairs that checked back. Elon’s flop+turn checks suggest a lot of his range is weak Kx/underpairs/missed broadways rather than a high frequency of flopped flushes (those hands often bet the flop). I’ll bet a constructive half-pot (200 into 400) to extract value while keeping the pot manageable out of position. If Elon check-raises big I’ll fold (that action is weighted to made clubs/sets); if he calls I’ll re-evaluate the river and exercise pot control if a club or other scary card comes.

T9o on this turn board (2c-Kc-7c-5h) remains complete air: no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw (T9 doesn't connect meaningfully with 2-5-7-K). Minni's preflop minraise from SB suggests a wide range, her flop check could indicate missed draw or medium strength, and the small 200 bet into 600 pot (1/3 pot) is often thin value (e.g., Kx, 7x, flush draw like AcQ) or a probe/bluff. Pot odds of 3:1 require ~25% equity, but I have near-zero equity against her continuing range. Calling commits to a bloated pot OOP with no plan for river; raising as a bluff is unattractive given the coordinated board (easy calls from draws/value) and her history of calling resistance. Folding preserves stack for better spots in a long 150-hand game, especially as villain shows aggression but folds to strength in spots like Hand 19/Hand 13.

Folded to me on the button with 7♦9♣ — a marginal but playable hand in position. With only the SB and BB left to act I want to seize the initiative and use positional advantage, so I’ll open-raise to a standard size (3x BB) to generate fold equity and isolate. A 300 raise is big enough to discourage some blind defenses but small relative to my deep stack. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet (hand lacks the equity for a big contest), call smaller defenses and play post-flop in position — c-bet favorable low/connected flops, check/float or pot-control on A/high boards. Avoid limp; raising maximizes long-term EV from this spot.

Jc2d is one of the worst possible hands in poker, completely uncoordinated offsuit with no high card value or playability. Facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB), from the small blind with poor post-flop position against the button and big blind, folding is the only logical play. Pot odds of roughly 1.8:1 aren't enough to justify calling with this trash hand, as equity against a typical button opening range is abysmal (<20%). No history suggests Minni is raising super wide enough to warrant a defense here, and speculative calls from blinds bleed chips long-term.

I am in the Big Blind with Q2o. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While I have a high stack and the pot odds are 2.25:1 to call, Q2o is a very weak hand to play out of position against a Button range that has been fairly aggressive. I have seen Minni raise or 3-bet frequently in previous hands, suggesting a wide opening range, but Q2o is at the bottom of my defending range and doesn't play well post-flop. I'll fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop on the button in a limped pot (SB likely limped by completing to 100, BB checked), pot 150, to call 100. 98o is a strong speculative hand in position with deep stacks. 3-handed dynamics favor wide button raises for value, isolation, or steals. Raising isolates weak limpers or takes it down outright. Standard sizing around 3x BB to 300. Postflop playability good IP multiway if called.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack of Spades and Four of Hearts. Elon has raised to 300 from the Button. This is a very weak hand to play out of position, and it does not have enough equity or post-flop playability to justify a call or a 3-bet bluff. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here.

I’m in the big blind with A♣3♦ facing a button open to 300 (pot 450, I must call 200). A3o is worth defending here: BTN opening range is wide, I get ~3.25:1 pot odds to call, and the ace blocker plus potential to hit an ace or backdoor draws post‑flop gives me good playability. A light 3‑bet is an option, but out of position with a marginal kicker I’d rather keep the pot manageable and see a flop. I’ll call to 300 total and proceed cautiously on the flop.

I have A♣3♦ — a pair of threes (bottom pair) on 3♠ 2♣ 9♠. This is a marginal made hand: decent showdown value versus a wide button opening but vulnerable to higher pairs and the two-spade board which gives many spade-draw combos. I'm out of position, so I want to control the pot and not bloat the pot with a weak pair. Checking keeps bluffs and weaker hands in so I can call a standard c-bet and re-evaluate on the turn. Plan: if he checks back I take a free turn card; if he bets small-to-medium (roughly ≤1/2 pot ≈ ≤325) I'll call to realize equity and keep the pot manageable; if he fires a large or polarized bet or check-raises heavily, I'll fold unless I pick up a clear read or improvement. Therefore I check here to control the pot and gather information.

Preflop button raise with 98o got called by BB Minni, who is out of position. Flop 3s 2c 9s gives me top pair with a weak 8 kicker. Board is drawy with a spade flush draw and some straight draws (e.g., 45 for OESD). As preflop aggressor in position, a continuation bet is standard for value against her wide BB calling range (Ax, pairs, suited connectors, draws) and to protect against draws. She checks, so betting denies equity to draws and charges them. Sizing around 60% pot (400 into 650) balances value and protection without overbetting vulnerable top pair. Deep stacks, but pot control not priority here. History shows small-medium bets common postflop.

I have A♣3♦ — a pair of treys with an Ace kicker on 3♠ 2♣ 9♠. BTN opened wide pre and c‑bet to 400 into a 1050 pot (~38% pot), which gives me good pot odds to call: 400 to win ~1450 (≈27–28% equity required). Versus a button c‑bet range I have meaningful showdown value, an Ace blocker, and backdoor equity, and I’m OOP so I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal one‑pair hand. Raising would be thin and likely dominated. I’ll call the 400 to see a turn and then re‑evaluate: fold to a large, polarized turn barrel if I don’t improve; call smaller turn bets; extract value or raise only if I make two pair/trips or get a clear read.

Check. I hold A♣3♦ — bottom pair (3s) with an Ace kicker. The 4♣ on the turn is a blank that doesn’t meaningfully improve my hand and I’m out of position with a marginal made hand. The pot is already 1,450 and Elon opened wide and c-bet the flop, so this has decent showdown value vs a wide range but is vulnerable to higher pairs and the spade draw. I want to control the pot size and keep bluffs/weaker hands in: if Elon bets a small-to-medium size (roughly ≤1/2 pot ≈ ≤725) I’ll call to realize equity; if he barrels large or jams (polarized) I’ll fold without improvement. If he checks back I take a free river and re-evaluate for thin value or a check-down. Overall checking is the lowest-risk, highest EV line here.

On the turn, the board is 3s 2c 9s 4c, giving me top pair with 9c8d (top pair, 8 kicker). The board is extremely drawy with spade flush draw, club flush draw, and straight draws (5x completes wheel straight with 2345). No flush has completed (only two spades and two clubs on board, impossible for opponent to have flush with two-hole cards). Minni checked flop and called my 400 c-bet OOP, then checked turn again. Her range includes draws, weaker pairs (77,88,TT-88), Ax suited, suited connectors. As preflop aggressor in position with deep stacks, I continue betting for thin value against her pairs/Ax and to charge/deny equity to all the live draws. Pot control is secondary with this vulnerable holding but good position. Sizing ~2/3 pot (1000 into 1450) balances value/protection, extracts from worse, and sets up river if called. Folding equity present vs pure draws. History shows Minni calls stations sometimes but folds to aggression (e.g., recent hands). Betting > checking.

I have A♣3♦ — bottom pair (3s) with an Ace kicker. Elon’s turn bet is 1,000 into the pot (pot shown 2,450; I must call 1,000). Calling requires roughly 29% equity (1,000/(2,450+1,000) ≈ 29%). My clean outs to make a hand that reliably beats value hands are very limited: remaining Aces (3) + remaining Threes (2) = 5 outs → 5/46 ≈ 11% to hit the river. Including 2s and 4s is optimistic (brings it to ~11/46 ≈ 24%) and many of those runouts still lose when Elon holds those cards. Elon’s sizing on the turn is large and consistent with a value-heavy, polarized line (9x, 4x, sets, strong two-pair), so I don’t have the required equity to call, and a raise as a bluff OOP is too thin and likely costly. Per my pre-turn plan to fold to a large, polarized barrel when unimproved, I fold and preserve chips for better spots.

I am on the button with Ace-Five offsuit (Ah 5c). This is a standard raising hand from late position. Both Minni and Elon have shown some aggression in previous hands, but with an Ace in hand and the button advantage, I should be opening for a raise to take control of the pot and potentially win it pre-flop. A standard 3x raise is appropriate here.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the SB with Ks7s — a suited king with decent playability and blockers, but out of position and somewhat dominated. Pot is 450 and it costs me 250 more (300 total) to continue. With deep stacks (~122bb) and a wide button opening range, calling to see a flop is the most +EV line: 3-betting the minimum to 500 gives little fold equity and commits OOP with a marginal hand, while folding is too tight vs a wide opener. I’ll call 300 total to see the flop and then re-evaluate vs Elon’s action (fold to a big 3-bet, proceed cautiously OOP vs multiple players).

2s9c is a trash hand offsuit, unconnected, poor playability. Facing button raise from Flash (likely wide range) and flat from Minni SB (decent speculative hands). Multiway OOP post-flop, my equity is low <20% vs calling ranges. Pot odds ~3.5:1 but not enough for this hand. Deep stacks, no need to speculate. Standard fold to preserve stack.

I have two pair (Kings and Sevens) on a K‑7‑3 flop heads‑up versus the button who opened wide. This is a strong but somewhat vulnerable hand (sets are possible but few combos since I hold a King and a Seven), so I want to extract value from worse Kx and 7x hands and deny free cards to any backdoor draws. Betting ~1/2 pot keeps the pot under control while getting called by worse hands — if Flash raises big I’ll re‑evaluate (large raises often indicate a set and require cautious play).

I raised to 300 pre-flop with Ace-Five offsuit and Minni called from the Small Blind. The flop comes King-Seven-Trey rainbow. Minni leads out (donk bets) for 350 into the 700 pot. This 'donk bet' on such a dry board often suggests she either connected with the King, has a middle pocket pair, or is testing my resolve as the pre-flop aggressor. While Ace-high has some showdown value, my kicker is weak, and I have no draws on this very dry board. If Minni has a King or even a Seven, I am drawing to only three outs (the remaining Aces). Given Minni's active and aggressive history in previous hands (like Hand #31 and Hand #14), she is unlikely to give up easily if I call or raise. I will fold here to preserve my stack and wait for a more favorable situation where I have a stronger hand or a draw to work with.

I’m on the button in a 3-handed pot with Q8s (Qc8c). This is a playable, suited queen with good postflop playability and positional advantage. I want to take the initiative, apply pressure to the blinds, and avoid playing passively out of position. Standard exploitative open-sizing in this spot is ~3x the big blind to maximize fold equity while keeping the pot manageable. I’ll raise to 300 total. If I face a 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate based on who it is and stack sizes; if called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and realize equity.

Preflop in the small blind facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB) from Minni, who is an aggressive raiser based on history (frequent opens and 3-bets). My hand Qs5c is offsuit, uncoordinated, and queen-high at best with poor playability. Defending range from SB should be tight; this doesn't make the cut. Pot odds to call 250 into 450 are ~1.8:1, insufficient for the equity needed against a button range. Folding preserves stack and avoids difficult postflop spots OOP with a trash hand. No 3-bet bluff incentive given position and opponent's tendencies.

I am in the Big Blind holding 9h 8h, which is a decent speculative hand. Minni raised to 300 from the Button. My pot odds are 200 to call into a 450 pot, meaning I need approximately 22% equity to continue. Against a standard Button opening range, 98o has enough playability and potential to justify a call, especially with deep stacks and the closing action. I will defend my blind and see a flop.

I have middle pair (Nines) with an eight kicker on a board of Ace-high with two clubs. My opponent, Minni, raised pre-flop from the Button, suggesting a wide range. On this texture, I will check to the aggressor to see how she responds. Middle pair is a decent bluff-catcher but not strong enough to lead out into the pre-flop raiser. I intend to check-call a reasonably sized bet to see if I can improve or if the board texture remains favorable for my hand.

I raised preflop and was called in the big blind; on the A♣ 9♦ 6♣ flop Flash checked. I hold Q♣8♣ — a strong four‑to‑a‑flush (9 outs, ~35% to make by the river) with decent positional advantage. This is a classic semi‑bluff spot: betting now gets fold equity against hands that missed the ace or one‑pair ranges, and if called my equity with the flush draw is attractive. I’ll make a half‑pot sized c‑bet (~350 into 650) to balance fold equity and keep the pot manageable. If I get raised heavily I’ll likely fold (they usually have a made Ace or better); if called I’ll continue selectively on the turn, especially when I improve or pick up additional equity.

I have middle pair (Nines) on an Ace-high board with a flush draw present. Minni's continuation bet of 350 into a 650 pot (about 54%) is standard for a pre-flop aggressor on this texture. While the Ace is well within her range, I have middle pair, which is too strong to fold immediately against a Button open. I will call to see the turn, evaluating if she continues her aggression on later streets. If she checks back the turn, I may have the best hand or can potentially bluff on certain rivers.

On the turn, I have improved to trips (Nines) on an Ace-high board. Since I am out of position against the pre-flop and flop aggressor (Minni), checking is the most effective play to induce a continuation bet or a bluff. My hand is very strong but not completely invulnerable to a flush draw, so I want to keep Minni's range as wide as possible. If she bets, I will evaluate whether to call and trap or check-raise for value based on her sizing.

I hold Qc8c — I had a 4‑to‑a‑flush on the flop and c‑bet, was called. Turn is 9d A c 6c 9h (board paired) and Flash checked. Pot is 1,350 and there’s no bet to call. With only one card to come I have 9 outs to a club (9/46 ≈ 19.6%) plus a few thin pair outs. The turn card that paired the board meaningfully reduces my fold equity: many hands that called the flop (Ax, pocket pairs, some clubs) will continue and won’t fold to a turn bluff very often. Betting here would be turning a pure draw into a bluff without enough fold equity, and it would bloat the pot against a calling/passive opponent. I’ll check to control the pot and realize my equity. Plan if action changes: if Flash leads the turn I’ll call very small bets (breakeven call threshold ≈ ≤435 chips given the 1,350 pot) but fold to larger turn bets; if he checks back I’ll go to the river and re‑evaluate — bet for value if I complete or have a good pair, otherwise fold to heavy river aggression.

On the river, I have trips (Nines with an Eight kicker). The board is 9d-Ac-6c-9h-Jd. After I check-called the flop, the turn (9h) went check-check, which suggests Minni might have an Ace she is trying to take to showdown or a pocket pair she's cautious with. By betting the river, I am targeting her Ax holdings or potentially a Jack that improved on the river. Checking again risks her checking back for a free showdown, missing out on value. A bet of approximately half the pot is likely to be called by an Ace or a Jack.

Board: 9♦ A♣ 6♣ 9♥ J♦. I hold Q♣8♣ — I missed my flush and have no pair (Q‑high). The pot is 2,050 and Flash bet 700; to call 700 to win 2,050 requires ≈34.1% equity. Given the line (I c‑bet flop, he check‑called; both checked the turn; he now makes a modest river bet), his range is heavily weighted toward value hands (A‑x, Jx, medium pairs) and is unlikely to fold enough to make a call or a bluff‑raise profitable. My actual showdown equity vs that value‑heavy range is far below the 34% breakeven threshold, and a river raise would risk a large chunk of chips with limited fold equity. For stack preservation and long‑term EV I fold here rather than call or bluff‑raise.

Preflop on the button with K3o after SB folds. Pot 150, to call 100 for BB. This is a standard spot to raise for a steal with position heads-up against the BB. K3o has a king blocker, decent high card playability, and we're deep-stacked. Minni defends wide but folds frequently to postflop aggression based on history. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) balances value and bluffs while building pot OOP.

I am in the Small Blind with 5c 6s. Elon (the Button) has raised to 3x (300). While 56o is a speculative connector, I am out of position against a player who has shown aggression in previous hands. Calling here would put me in a difficult spot post-flop, and raising (3-betting) as a bluff with this hand isn't ideal given the stack sizes and the likelihood of being called or 4-bet. Folding is the most disciplined and standard play in this position to protect my stack.

I’m in the big blind with T♥8♠ vs a standard Button open to 300. I only need 200 more (300 total) into a 450 pot, so the pot odds and implied odds justify defending. T8o is marginal and not a great 3-bet candidate (especially out of position and unsuited) — a light 3-bet here invites 4-bets and puts me in a tough spot. I’ll call to see a flop, plan to play cautiously out of position, and fold to a large 4-bet or heavy postflop aggression if I don’t improve.

I called preflop with T♥8♠ and the flop comes 8♦ 2♥ K♦ — I have middle pair (eights) which is a decent but vulnerable holding against a preflop raiser. Elon checked the flop, which can indicate either weakness or pot-control with Kx. Out of position I don’t want to inflate the pot with a medium-strength hand that can be crushed by Kx or sets and can be exploited by raises. I’ll control the pot and induce bluffs by checking. Plan: call a reasonable small bet on later streets to see showdown, but fold to large aggression or if the board develops (a K or a dangerous diamond) that clearly beats me.

Flop is Kd 8d 2h, giving me top pair top kicker (Kh pairs Kd) on a relatively dry board with two diamonds and a low heart. Minni checked after calling preflop from BB, likely weak range including some draws, worse kings, or floats. With position and initiative, this is a clear value betting spot to build the pot, protect against diamond/straight draws (gutshots like QJT), and charge her wide calling range. History shows Minni folds often to postflop bets, but she calls light sometimes, so value bet half-pot sizing ~325, but sizing up to 400 for protection and value maximization given deep stacks.

I checked the flop with middle pair (T8o; pair of eights) and Elon fired a small continuation bet of 400 into the 1050 pot. That bet gives me ~27% pot odds to continue and my hand is often good vs a c-bet range (bluffs, Kx floats, some draws). Out of position I don’t want to inflate the pot with a vulnerable one-pair hand or turn it into a big bluffing war, so calling is the lowest-variance play to realize equity and re-evaluate on the turn. I’ll fold to heavy turn aggression or a clear K/diamond that improves his range, but otherwise plan to see one more card and proceed cautiously.

I have T♥8♠ — middle pair (eights) with a weak kicker on 8♦ 2♥ K♦ 6♥. Pot is 1450 and there is no bet to call. Elon was the preflop raiser and c-bet the flop (400) which I called; that line can be Kx, a float, or a wide c-bet. Betting out of position here bloats the pot and puts me in a tough spot vs Kx, sets or overpairs that will often raise or call and keep me committed with a weak kicker. Checking controls the pot, allows me to realize equity, and keeps bluffs in his range so I can call a reasonable river bet. I’ll check and re-evaluate on the river — fold to very large aggression that clearly represents K/sets, but call modest river bets for showdown.

Turn board brings 6h, adding a heart flush draw to the existing diamond draw and possible gutshots. Board: Kd8d2h6h remains somewhat drawy but my TPTK (Kh) is still very strong, ahead of most of Minni's calling range from flop (worse Kx like KQ/KJ/KT, pairs like 88/22, draws like flush/straight draws). She checks again, showing weakness, and history indicates she folds often to continued aggression but calls light with marginal hands. With position and deep stacks (her 11k, me 9.5k), this is a prime value betting spot to charge draws and extract from worse made hands. Betting ~2/3 pot (1000) balances protection against equity and maximizes value while keeping pot manageable if she shoves.

Fold. I have only middle pair (8s) with a weak T kicker out of position on 8♦ 2♥ K♦ 6♥. Elon’s line (preflop open → small flop c-bet → large turn bet of 1,000 into ~1,450, pot now 2,450) is a strong-value line representing Kx, big pairs/overpairs or sets; it could be a polarized bluff but is much more likely value here. Calling 1,000 to win 3,450 requires ~29% equity — my hand is unlikely to have that vs his continuing range and I’m likely to face more river aggression. Raising as a bluff OOP is too risky and thin. Consistent with my plan to fold to heavy turn aggression, I’ll conserve chips and fold this marginal hand.

I am on the button with 9s 4c. This is a very weak hand with poor connectivity and no high-card value. In a 3-handed game, standard opening ranges from the button are wide, but 94 offsuit is well below the threshold for a profitable open. Folding is the most prudent play to conserve my stack, especially since I am currently the chip leader.

Heads-up from the small blind (I’m also the button postflop) — so I’ll have position after the flop. K5s (K♥5♥) is a reasonable opening hand heads-up: decent high-card strength, suitedness and some backdoor/flush potential. With ~113BB effective I want to be the aggressor and not give the big blind a cheap price to see flops. A standard, non-minimum open to ~3x the BB keeps our range strong and reduces cold-callers/3-betters. Plan: raise to 300 total to apply pressure; if Elon folds we scoop the 150 pot, if he calls I’ll play postflop in position and c-bet/frequency-adjust depending on the board; if he 3-bets large I will fold this marginal holding.

Facing a standard 3BB raise from the SB after BTN folds. Minni is aggressive and raises frequently from SB with a wide range based on hand history (many unopposed steals). Jd6s is offsuit but has a Jack blocker and some straight potential. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into 400 pot (~3:1, requiring ~25% equity). Against a wide SB opening range (~50-60% hands), J6o has around 40%+ equity and decent playability post-flop (top pair potential, gutshots). Defending BB wide OOP vs loose SB open is standard. Will see flop and play in position advantage post-flop. No fold equity for 3-bet with this hand; calling maximizes EV deep-stacked.

I’m the preflop raiser and have position with K♥5♥ on Js 8♥ 3♠. This is a fairly dry flop that favors the raiser’s range — I have king‑high plus a weak backdoor heart draw. C‑betting here applies pressure, denies equity to many of Elon’s unpaired hands, and charges any spade/straight draws. I’ll make a standard ~1/2 pot continuation bet (300). Plan: if he folds I scoop; if he calls I proceed cautiously — continue barreling only with a heart or a clear improvement, and fold to a large, committed raise with just K‑high.

Facing a standard c-bet of 300 into 900 (~1/3 pot) from aggressive SB Minni after her wide pre-flop open. Board Js8h3s is somewhat drawy with two spades and possible gutshots (e.g., T9). My Jd6s hits top pair with a weak 6 kicker, plus backdoor spade flush draw. Against her polarized SB range (value like AJ+, sets, or bluffs/draws), top pair has strong equity (~55-65% vs typical c-bet range based on history of wide aggression). Pot odds are excellent (300 into 900, ~4:1, needing ~20% equity). Calling OOP keeps pot small, realizes equity, and allows turn control or check-raise if needed. Raising risks getting coolered by better Js or overpairs while bloating pot OOP with marginal kicker; folding too tight given strength and history (Minni cbets frequently wide, e.g., Hands 13-40 shows loose play). Plan: call, evaluate turn (implied odds deep-stacked ~100BB).

Turn A♣ is a clear scare card that substantially improves the caller’s range (A‑x, some pairs, two‑pair/set possibilities) and it also kills my backdoor heart plan — with the turn being a club I can no longer make a flush on the river with K♥5♥. I have no pair and only K‑high, so continuing to barrel here would be thin and easily exploitable. I opened and c‑bet the flop, but per my plan I only continue barreling on a turn when I have a heart or a clear improvement — I don’t. Checking controls the pot, keeps bluffs in his range, and lets me fold to pressure without bloating the pot with air. If Elon bets very small (a token probe, ~≤1/4 pot) I may consider calling to induce river play, but I will fold to any sizable stab (≥~1/3–1/2 pot) since K‑high is a poor call. For now: check and re‑evaluate on the river.

Turn board brings the Ac, pairing the top card potential but scaring off weaker hands and improving many in Minni's SB c-bet range (Ax, broadway draws). My Jd6s is now top pair weak kicker (J6) on an Ace-high board, with showdown value against her bluffs/missed spade draws/gutshots from flop (e.g., T9s, 75s, 45). She cbets flop wide (consistent with aggressive history: Hands 13,14,15,17,19,30,31,35,37-40 show frequent SB aggression/cbets) but checks turn OOP—likely pot control with marginal made hands (mid pairs, weak Ax) or giving up with air/draws. Pot 1200 deep (~100BB), excellent implied odds unnecessary since hand is capped. Betting risks raising her fold equity with better Js/Ax or inducing bluffs/raises OOP; thin value minimal vs polarized range. Checking realizes equity, controls pot OOP, induces bluffs on river (common vs checked-to ranges), and free showdown if she checks again (frequent in history when weak, e.g., Hands 15,22,34). Plan: check, evaluate river bet size/history for call/fold (stack her Ax/Jx). High EV pot control play.

I have K♥5♥ and never improved — K‑high only. We c‑bet the flop and checked the turn when the A♣ hit; Elon’s flop call and turn check mean his range still contains many hands that beat K‑high (A‑x, Jx, 8x, pocket pairs/sets). The river paired the board (8), which further helps his range and makes a bluff less credible. Betting here would risk building a pot with a very weak hand and is unlikely to fold enough better hands to be +EV. With 1200 in the pot and ~10k effective stacks, the prudent +EV line is to check and go to showdown (or fold to a river bet), preserving stack and avoiding unnecessary speculation.

River action IP after Minni checks OOP on a paired 8s board (Js8h3s Ac8c). My Jd6s gives top pair (Js) with weak 6 kicker, blocking some J8 two-pair combos. Her line: wide SB raise pre (aggressive history: frequent SB opens/cbets in hands 13-40), small flop cbet on spade-drawy board (Js8h3s hits broadway/draws), check Ac turn (blocker/scare card improves Ax/broadway), check river 8c (pairs board, improves 8x trips/two-pair). Checking range polarized/capped OOP: weak Ax (A9-AQ no kicker showdown), unpaired midpairs (87-97, Jx low), missed draws (spade flush T9s-KQs, straight draws). Strong hands like 88 trips, J8/A8 two-pair, sets less likely to check-check turn-river OOP without betting value. Deep stacks (~100BB), pot 1200. Betting ~1/2 pot (600) extracts thin value from calling stations with worse (weak Ax, midpairs), fold equity vs pure air/draws (high % of range), protection vs draws (though river paired, backdoors possible). Checking realizes equity safely but misses value from worse hands that showdown/fold to bet; EV higher to bet IP vs loose-aggressive OOP villain who cbets wide but slows down with marginal strength (e.g., hands 15,19,37 check weak). Plan: pot control if raised, stack-off only vs shove with better.

I never improved with K♥5♥ — I only have king‑high. Elon called my flop c‑bet and both of us checked the A♣ on the turn, which strongly favors his calling range (Jx, Ax, pocket pairs, or slow‑played big hands). The river paired the 8, further improving many hands that beat K‑high. He bets 600 into the pot (pot was 1200 pre‑bet, now 1800); calling 600 would require roughly 25% equity to break even. I’m a very poor bluff‑catcher here and have no realistic outs; his line is much more value‑heavy than bluff‑heavy. With deep stacks and plenty of future hands to pick spots, folding and preserving my stack is the +EV play.

Button with T6o — marginal but acceptable to open on the button against just SB and BB. Pot is 150 and to call is 100; my normal open size at this table has been ~3xBB, which applies pressure and keeps my stealing range wide. I raise to 300 to take the initiative and play in position; plan to continuation-bet favorable flops and fold to heavy aggression or a sizable 3-bet (T6o is easy to release out of position).

3-handed vs button open to 3BB. Minni on button likely raising wide (~50%+ range). J9o has solid equity (~42-45% vs typical BTN open range), good playability for straights/flushes potential, and blockers to Jx hands. Pot odds excellent (calling 250 into 450, ~2.8:1 direct + implied). Deep stacks allow postflop maneuverability despite OOP. Folding too tight; prefer speculative call to realize equity and pot control opportunities, especially if BB folds.

I am in the Big Blind with T4o, which is a weak hand. Minni has raised to 3x from the button and Elon has called from the Small Blind. I am getting 3.5:1 on a call, but T4o is poorly connected and likely to be dominated or difficult to play out of position against two opponents. Given the previous hands, both Minni and Elon play relatively aggressively post-flop. I'll choose to fold here and wait for a better spot to enter the pot.

OOP in SB vs button raiser heads-up on Kc5d8d flop with J9o (no pair, no draw, jack-high air). Board favors Minni's wide button opening range heavily (Kx, Ax, pairs like 77/66/99, suited connectors drawing to straights/flush). My equity is low (~25-35% vs her range), playability poor multi-street OOP with no showdown value or draws. Betting is a bluff into a capping station likely to call/raise wide; check-raise suicidal without equity. Best is check-fold to c-bet (expected ~50-70% pot), preserving stack. If miraculously checked behind, take free river card. Consistent with disciplined postflop play to realize preflop equity realization without over-realizing into disaster.

I opened to 300 on the button with T6o and took the initiative. Flop Kc 5d 8d misses my hand, but heads-up vs Elon's calling range there are lots of overcards, weak Aces and small pairs that will fold to pressure. Elon checked, so this is a good spot for a compact continuation bet to pick up the pot and charge the hands that have any equity. I size to ~300 (small-to-medium, ~40% pot) to keep fold equity while not overcommitting with a pure bluff. Plan: fold to a sizable check-raise, and if called use position to control the turn — fire again on safe blanks or surrender to heavy turn aggression.

Facing a small continuation bet of 300 into 1000 pot (3.3:1 odds) OOP with J9o on Kc5d8d board. Hand is jack-high with no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw. Equity vs Minni's button c-bet range (~30-40% at best, often lower OOP) is insufficient to call profitably considering reverse implied odds and future streets. Raising as a bluff is poor: small bet size invites calls from her wide range (Kx, pairs 66-99, AdXd, suited connectors), and pot-committing OOP is disastrous if she has any equity. Check-fold preserves stack for better spots; consistent with GTO and exploitative play vs aggressive button who cbets frequently when checked to (per history). Long-term EV positive by avoiding thin calls.

8h3d is a trash hand: offsuit, low, gapped, no connectivity or high card value. On the button with position, but facing a 100 to call into 250 pot (2.5:1), equity is poor against any reasonable range. Opponents are aggressive but no need to speculate with this. Folding preserves stack for premium spots in this long game.

I am in the Small Blind with 9h 6c. After the Button folds, the action is on me. 96o is a weak hand to play out of position, even in a blind-versus-blind scenario. Minni (the Big Blind) has shown frequent aggression in recent hands (e.g., Hand 32 where she 3-bet my SB raise). Given the low quality of my hand and the disadvantage of acting first on every post-flop street, I will choose the low-variance route and fold.

Holding Ten of Spades and Five of Diamonds (T5o) on the button, this hand is significantly below the threshold for a standard opening range even in a short-handed game. Minni and Elon have shown willingness to defend and play aggressively. There is no strategic advantage to playing such a weak hand out of position post-flop if called, or facing a potential 3-bet. Folding is the most disciplined and profitable long-term play.

Heads-up unopened pot from the small blind with A♠4♠. This is a playable, suited ace with good heads-up equity and useful blockers to premium Axs hands. I want the initiative and some fold equity rather than completing and playing a marginal hand OOP. Table norms show many opens to ~300, so I’ll open to 300 (3xBB) — a standard size that’s small relative to our ~11k stack but enough to apply pressure on Elon. Plan: if he folds I pick up the pot; if he calls I’ll continuation-bet frequently on dry, uncoordinated flops and play more cautiously on wet or Ace-high boards. If he 3-bets large (e.g. >~600–700) I’ll usually fold OOP with A4s; vs a small 3-bet I may consider a call depending on sizing and read. Overall 3x open maximizes fold equity and keeps postflop options flexible.

QTo offsuit in BB facing a standard 3x raise from SB after UTG fold. Minni has been aggressive in recent hands, raising frequently from SB/early position. Pot odds are excellent (calling 200 into 400, about 3:1 implied). QTo has decent equity against a wide SB opening range (~40-50% equity OOP) and good playability for multiway or heads-up pots. Defending wide here is standard to realize equity and counter aggression. Folding is too tight; 3-betting is too strong for offsuit QTo. Will call and play postflop cautiously OOP, looking to float or hit.

I have A♠4♠ on 2♠-4♥-5♣: middle pair with the nut Ace kicker and a backdoor spade. This is a very favorable flop for a continuation bet — dry, low and unlikely to have hit the BB’s calling range hard. As preflop aggressor I want to extract value from worse hands (A‑x, K‑x, Q‑x, smaller pairs) and fold out pure overcards while protecting my hand against turn cards that give my opponent equity. I’ll size to ~1/2 pot (300) — big enough to get folds and deny equity but small enough to keep worse hands and floating hands in to extract more value on later streets. If called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (proceed cautiously versus aggression; fold to large check‑raises that represent sets/55/22).

QTo offsuit completely misses the 2s4h5c flop: queen-high with no pair, no draws, just backdoor potential at best. Facing Minni's half-pot c-bet in a heads-up pot OOP. Her SB opening range is wide and aggressive per history (frequent raises/folds), but this board favors her value range (sets, two-pair, overpairs, combo draws like 36/A3) and her c-bets are likely wide but profitable to defend selectively. Pot odds ~3:1 but implied odds poor OOP with no equity; folding realizes we defended wide pre-flop correctly but avoids spew post-flop. Raising as a bluff is unbalanced and risky deep-stacked OOP vs aggressive opponent who may continue.

4♥9♣ on the button in a 3-handed pot is a weak, easily dominated hand. The cost to see a flop is only 100 (1 BB) against deep stacks, so calling preserves position and keeps the pot small while giving me a chance to hit. Raising to 200 as a steal is doable in some spots, but with Elon showing frequent preflop aggression a small raise risks a 3-bet and difficult decisions out of position. Plan: call 100, evaluate the flop — fold to significant pressure with a dry miss, but be prepared to c-bet or apply pressure if I connect or pick up good equity/draws.

3-handed pre-flop in SB facing a button limp. JQo is a strong hand in this spot with good playability, high card strength, and suited potential but offsuit is still fine deepstacked. Button limps suggest a wide range including speculative hands. Raising builds pot for value, takes initiative, and applies pressure on BB. Standard raise size to 3x BB (300 total) after limp. Folding or limping OOP is suboptimal.

Hand #45 is at the Pre-flop stage. I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Eight offsuit. Minni (Button) limped for 100, and Elon (Small Blind) raised to 300. While the pot odds are reasonable (200 to call into 500), Ten-Eight offsuit is a weak hand that does not play well out of position in a potentially three-way pot. Given Elon's aggression and the high probability of being dominated or having poor equity realization post-flop, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my chip lead.

I’m on the button with 4♥9♣ — a marginal, easily dominated hand. Elon (SB) re-raised to 300 after I limped, leaving me to call 200 more. Given the stacks (~11k effective) and the pot size, calling is cheap and preserves position versus an opponent who raises frequently. I don’t want to 3‑bet with this raggy offsuit hand (too often dominated and awkward postflop), and folding surrenders too much value for a small price. Plan: call to see a flop; continue only with decent pair/draws or a favorable texture, otherwise fold to heavy continuation aggression. If I hit a pair or strong draw I’ll use position to apply pressure; on dry boards I’ll concede to large bets to keep losses small.

Flop is 2c-6s-3h, a low rainbow board that completely misses my JQo offsuit. I have no pair, no draw, and only overcards for showdown value. Preflop, my SB raise after button limp with JQo was standard for initiative and playability in 3-handed deepstack play. Minni's limp-call range from button is very wide (suited connectors, Ax, small pairs, broadways). This board hits her speculative range more often than mine (small pairs like 22-66, 45, 34, A-high floats). Betting continuation here as first to act OOP risks getting called by better hands or raised off equity. Check preserves my stack, allows pot control, realizes fold equity on her checks (if weak), and keeps the hand multi-street if checked to. If she bets, I can fold cleanly. No need to bluff-raise preflop equity or value here with air. Deep stacks favor checking and evaluating her range further.

Elon checked to me on 2c-6s-3h. I hold 4h-9c — no pair, only a 4‑out (a 5 gives me 2-3-4-5-6) and a meaningless backdoor heart. With such poor showdown equity I’m not trying to see a cheap turn; I want to apply pressure and take the pot now.
